verses.mistral: We dare not commend ourselves to anyone's praise, but let the one who commends us be an approved worker by you, and let the one who is commended be as an approved worker by God. For it is written: "They will not hunger or thirst, but their souls will be well-nourished," as the Lord says: "They are the people for whom I am concerned." But we have become foolish to the Galatians! You see, you are waiting for proof of our Christ, yet we are asserting that we have already proved him to you. It is not just a matter of words; it's about demonstrating them in action, proving ourselves genuine before God. So we commend ourselves not to you again but to God who judges us. And hope lies within this: if we have been judged by the one whom we are commending ourselves, then we have proven ourselves acceptable to him, whether it is in your eyes or God's. 2 Corinthians 10:12 (previous verses for context) - "This be reckoned by such a one, that as we are in the word (letter) sent through epistles, so also we are in the deed when present."
